title: Electronic mail user interface
abstract: The present disclosure relates to electronic mail user interfaces. A device, including a display, a touch-sensitive surface, and a rotatable input mechanism, is described in relation to accessing, composing, and manipulating electronic mail. In some examples, a user can provide input through the rotatable input mechanism to access a landing screen of an electronic mail application. The landing screen concurrently displays an affordance for accessing an electronic mail inbox and an affordance for accessing an interface for composing electronic mail.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: at an electronic device with a display, a touch-sensitive surface, and a rotatable input mechanism: displaying, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receiving user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, displaying a landing screen of the electronic mail application, wherein the landing screen includes display of an inbox affordance and a compose affordance; receiving user input associated with the landing screen; determining whether the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance or a touch associated with the displayed compose affordance; in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance, displaying, on the display, a list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated the displayed compose affordance, displaying, on the display, an interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying, on the 

 display, a list of electronic mail message objects associated with the electronic mail thread object. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture from a bezel of the electronic device at the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture from the bezel of the electronic device at the list of electronic mail message objects, replacing display of the list of electronic mail message objects with the list of electronic mail thread objects. 4. The method of any of claims 2-3, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects, displaying, on the display, an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 5. The method of claim 4, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture from a bezel of the electronic device associated with the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture from the bezel of the electronic device associated with the electronic mail message, replacing display of the electronic mail message with the list of electronic mail message objects. 6. The method of any of claims 4-5, wherein the touch- sensitive surface is configured to detect intensity of touches, and the method further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch on the electronic mail message; determining whether a characteristic intensity of the touch on the electronic mail message is above an intensity threshold; and in response to receiving the user input representing the touch on the electronic mail message and in accordance with a determination that the characteristic intensity of the 

 touch is above the intensity threshold, displaying, on the display, a first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein displaying the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message comprises replacing display of the electronic mail message with display of the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 8. The method of any of claims 6-7, wherein the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message on a second device, and a first affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the first affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the first affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message. 10. The method of any of claims 8-9, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with a second set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the second set of affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message, and a second affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the second affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the second affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of the first set of one or more affordances. 13. The method of any of claims 11-12, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for replying to the electronic mail message. 14. The method of any of claims 11-12, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message. 15. The method of any of claims 11-14, further comprising: 

 receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for forwarding the electronic mail message. 16. The method of any of claims 8-15, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with a third set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the third set of affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message, and a third affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the third affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the third affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the first set of one or more affordances. 19. The method of any of claims 17-18, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message; and 

 in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message, including a flag indicator. 20. The method of any of claims 17-19, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message, including an unread indicator. 21. The method of any of claims 17-19, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message, moving the electronic mail message into an archive folder. 22. The method of any of claims 17-21, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message, causing the electronic mail message to be displayed on a linked companion device. 23. The method of any of claims 1-22, wherein the interface for composing the new electronic mail message comprises one or more of: an affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message, an affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message, and 

 an affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message, replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a fourth set of one or more affordances associated with the new electronic mail message. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the fourth set of one or more affordances associated with the new electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with recent contacts, an affordance associated with dictation, and an affordance associated with a keyboard. 26. The method of claim 25, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with recent contacts; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with recent contacts, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of one or more recent contact affordances. 27. The method of claim 26, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with a recent contact affordance of the one or more recent contact affordances, the recent contact affordance associated with an email address; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the recent contact affordance, adding the email address as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 28. The method of claim 25, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with dictation; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with dictation, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of a dictation interface. 29. The method of claim 28, further comprising: receiving user input representing recipient speech; and in response to receiving user input representing recipient speech: transcribing the user input representing recipient speech, and adding the transcribed recipient speech as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 30. The method of claim 25, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with the keyboard; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with the keyboard, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of one or more keys of a keyboard. 31. The method of claim 30, further comprising: receiving user input representing keyboard input using the one or more keys of the keyboard, the user input representing keyboard input associated with an email address; and in response to receiving user input representing the keyboard input, adding the email address as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 32. The method of any of claims 23-31, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message, 

 replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a dictation interface. 33. The method of claim 32, further comprising: receiving user input representing subject speech; and in response to receiving user input representing subject speech: transcribing the user input representing subject speech, and adding the transcribed subject speech as subject information to the new electronic mail message. 34. The method of any of claims 23-33, further comprising: receiving data representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving data representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message, replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a dictation interface. 35. The method of claim 34, further comprising: receiving user input representing body speech; and in response to receiving user input representing body speech: transcribing the user input representing body speech, and adding the transcribed body speech as body information to the new electronic mail message. 36. The method of any of claims 1-35, further comprising: determining whether the new electronic mail message includes recipient information, subject information, and body information; and in response to determining that the new electronic mail message includes recipient information, subject information, and body information, displaying an affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising: 

 receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message, transmitting the new electronic mail message. 38. The method of any of claims 1-37, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially downward swipe gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially downward swipe gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying an affordance for composing a new electronic mail message. 39. The method of any of claims 1-38, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail thread associated with the electronic mail thread object. 40. The method of any of claims 2-39, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 41. The method of any of claims 2-40, further comprising: 

 receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; determining whether the user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object indicates the swipe gesture exceeds a first threshold or exceeds a second threshold; in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object and in accordance with a determination that the user input indicates the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object exceeds the first threshold and does not exceed the second threshold, displaying an affordance for marking an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object and in accordance with a determination that the user input indicates the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object exceeds the second threshold, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 42. A method, comprising: at an electronic device with a display, a touch-sensitive surface, and a rotatable input mechanism: displaying, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receiving user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, displaying a list of electronic mail thread objects; receiving user input representing a swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input representing the swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying an affordance for composing a new electronic mail message. 43. The method of claim 42, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance for composing a new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance for composing a new electronic mail message, displaying, on the display, an interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein displaying the interface for composing a new electronic mail message comprises replacing display of the list of electronic mail thread objects with display of the interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 45. The method of any of claims 42-44, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying, on the display, a list of electronic mail message objects associated with the electronic mail thread object. 46. The method of claim 45, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture from a bezel of the electronic device at the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture from the bezel of the electronic device at the list of electronic mail message objects, replacing display of the list of electronic mail message objects with the list of electronic mail thread objects. 47. The method of any of claims 45-46, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects, displaying, on the display, an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 48. The method of claim 47, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture from a bezel of the electronic device associated with the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture from the bezel of the electronic device associated with the electronic mail message, replacing display of the electronic mail message with the list of electronic mail message objects. 49. The method of any of claims 47-48, wherein the touch- sensitive surface is configured to detect intensity of touches, and the method further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch on the electronic mail message; determining whether a characteristic intensity of the touch on the electronic mail message is above an intensity threshold; and in response to receiving the user input representing the touch on the electronic mail message and in accordance with a determination that the characteristic intensity of the touch is above the intensity threshold: displaying, on the display, a first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein displaying the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message comprises replacing display of the electronic mail message with display of the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 51. The method of any of claims 49-50, wherein the first set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message on a second device, and a first affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 52. The method of claim 51, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the first affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the first affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message. 53. The method of any of claims 51-52, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the electronic mail message, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with a second set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 54. The method of claim 53, wherein the second set of affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message, and a second affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 55. The method of claim 54, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the second affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the second affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of the first set of one or more affordances. The method of any of claims 54-55, further comprising: 

 receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to the sender of the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for replying to the electronic mail message. 57. The method of any of claims 54-56, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for replying to all recipients of the electronic mail message. 58. The method of any of claims 54-57, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with forwarding the electronic mail message, replacing display of the second set of one or more affordances with display of an interface for forwarding the electronic mail message. 59. The method of any of claims 51-58, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with marking the electronic mail message, replacing display of the first set of one or more affordances with a third set of one or more affordances associated with the electronic mail message. 60. The method of claim 59, wherein the third set of affordances associated with the electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message, 

 an affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message, an affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message, and a third affordance associated with a cancel action associated with the electronic mail message. 61. The method of claim 60, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the third affordance associated with the cancel action; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the third affordance associated with the cancel action, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the first set of one or more affordances. 62. The method of any of claims 60-61, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with flagging the electronic mail message, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message, including a flag indicator. 63. The method of any of claims 60-62, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with marking as unread the electronic mail message, replacing display of the third set of one or more affordances with display of the electronic mail message, including an unread indicator. 64. The method of any of claims 60-63, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with archiving the electronic mail message, moving the electronic mail message into an archive folder. 65. The method of any of claims 51-64, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with opening the electronic mail message, causing the electronic mail message to be displayed on a linked companion device. 66. The method of any of claims 43-65, wherein the interface for composing the new electronic mail message comprises one or more of: an affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message, an affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message, and an affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message. 67. The method of claim 66, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding recipient information to the new electronic mail message, replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a fourth set of one or more affordances associated with the new electronic mail message. 68. The method of claim 67, wherein the fourth set of one or more affordances associated with the new electronic mail message includes one or more of: an affordance associated with recent contacts, an affordance associated with dictation, and an affordance associated with a keyboard. 69. The method of claim 68, further comprising: 

 receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with recent contacts; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with recent contacts, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of one or more recent contact affordances. 70. The method of claim 69, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with a recent contact affordance of the one or more recent contact affordances, the recent contact affordance associated with an email address; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the recent contact affordance, adding the email address as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 71. The method of claim 68, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with dictation; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with dictation, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of a dictation interface. 72. The method of claim 71, further comprising: receiving user input representing recipient speech; and in response to receiving user input representing recipient speech: transcribing the user input representing recipient speech, and adding the transcribed recipient speech as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 73. The method of claim 68, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with the keyboard; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with the keyboard, replacing display of the fourth set of one or more affordances with display of one or more keys of a keyboard. 74. The method of claim 73, further comprising: receiving user input representing keyboard input using the one or more keys of the keyboard, the user input representing keyboard input associated with an email address; and in response to receiving user input representing the keyboard input, adding the email address as recipient information to the new electronic mail message. 75. The method of any of claims 66-74, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding subject information to the new electronic mail message, replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a dictation interface. 76. The method of claim 75, further comprising: receiving user input representing subject speech; and in response to receiving user input representing subject speech: transcribing the user input representing subject speech, and adding the transcribed subject speech as subject information to the new electronic mail message. 77. The method of any of claims 66-76, further comprising: receiving data representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving data representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with adding body information to the new electronic mail message, replacing display of the interface for composing the new electronic mail message with display of a dictation interface. 78. The method of claim 77, further comprising: receiving user input representing body speech; and in response to receiving user input representing body speech: 

 transcribing the user input representing body speech, and adding the transcribed body speech as body information to the new electronic mail message. 79. The method of any of claims 43-78, further comprising: determining whether the new electronic mail message includes recipient information, subject information, and body information; and in response to determining that the new electronic mail message includes recipient information, subject information, and body information, displaying an affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message. 80. The method of claim 79, further comprising: receiving user input representing a touch associated with the affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message; and in response to receiving user input representing the touch associated with the affordance associated with sending the new electronic mail message, transmitting the new electronic mail message. 81. The method of any of claims 42-80, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail thread object of the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail thread associated with the electronic mail thread object. 82. The method of any of claims 47-81, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail 

 message objects, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 83. The method of any of claims 47-82, further comprising: receiving user input representing a substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with an electronic mail message object of the list of electronic mail message objects; determining whether the user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object indicates the swipe gesture exceeds a first threshold or exceeds a second threshold; in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object and in accordance with a determination that the user input indicates the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object exceeds the first threshold and does not exceed the second threshold, displaying an affordance for marking an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object; and in response to receiving user input representing the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object and in accordance with a determination that the user input indicates the substantially horizontal swipe gesture associated with the electronic mail message object exceeds the second threshold, displaying an affordance for deleting an electronic mail message associated with the electronic mail message object. 84. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions for performing the method of any of claims 1-83. 85. A system comprising: the non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 84; and one or more processors capable of executing the instructions of the non-transitory computer readable storage medium. 86. A system comprising means for performing the method of any of claims 1-83. 87. A system comprising: a display; a touch- sensitive surface; a rotatable input mechanism; means for displaying, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; means for receiving user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; means responsive to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, for displaying a landing screen of the electronic mail application, wherein the landing screen includes display of an inbox affordance and a compose affordance; means for receiving user input associated with the landing screen; means for determining whether the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance or a touch associated with the displayed compose affordance; means responsive to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance, for displaying, on the display, a list of electronic mail thread objects; and means response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated the displayed compose affordance, for displaying, on the display, an interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 88. A system comprising: a display; a touch- sensitive surface; a rotatable input mechanism; 

 means for displaying, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; means for receiving user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; means responsive to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, for displaying a list of electronic mail thread objects; means for receiving user input representing a swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects; and means responsive to receiving the user input representing the swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects, for displaying an affordance for composing a new electronic mail message. 89. An electronic device, comprising: a display unit; a touch- sensitive surface unit; a rotatable input mechanism unit; and a processing unit coupled to the display unit, the touch-sensitive surface unit, and the rotatable input mechanism unit, the processing unit configured to: enable display of an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receive user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism unit; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism unit, enable display of a landing screen of the electronic mail application, wherein the landing screen includes display of an inbox affordance and a compose affordance; receive user input associated with the landing screen; determine whether the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance or a touch associated with the displayed compose affordance; 

 in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance, enable display on the display unit, a list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated the displayed compose affordance, enable display on the display unit, an interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 90. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing one or more programs, the one or more programs comprising instructions, which when executed by one or more processors of an electronic device with a display, a touch-sensitive surface, and a rotatable input mechanism, cause the device to: display, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receive user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, display a landing screen of the electronic mail application, wherein the landing screen includes display of an inbox affordance and a compose affordance; receive user input associated with the landing screen; determine whether the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance or a touch associated with the displayed compose affordance; in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated with the displayed inbox affordance, display, on the display, a list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input associated with the landing screen and in accordance with a determination that the user input associated with the landing screen represents a touch associated the displayed compose affordance, display, on the display, an interface for composing a new electronic mail message. 174. An electronic device, comprising: a display; a touch- sensitive surface; 

 a rotatable input mechanism one or more processors; a memory; and one or more programs, wherein the one or more programs are stored in the memory and configured to be executed by the one or more processors, the one or more programs including instructions for: displaying, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receiving user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, displaying a list of electronic mail thread objects; receiving user input representing a swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input representing the swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects, displaying an affordance for composing a new electronic mail message. 175. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing one or more programs, the one or more programs comprising instructions, which when executed by one or more processors of an electronic device with a display, a touch-sensitive surface, and a rotatable input mechanism, cause the device to: display, on the display, an affordance representing an electronic mail application, wherein the affordance representing the electronic mail application includes an indication of a number of unread electronic mail messages of the electronic mail application; receive user input representing movement of the rotatable input mechanism; in response to receiving the user input representing the movement of the rotatable input mechanism, display a list of electronic mail thread objects; receive user input representing a swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects; and in response to receiving the user input representing the swipe down gesture associated with the list of electronic mail thread objects, display an affordance for composing a new electronic mail message.
